response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test frameworks and libraries — but the core required stack is largely absent. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no demonstrated deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for this role.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments)
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput systems
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin or JVM-internals experience; Java appears only peripherally
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework background
Primary expertise (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js) is orthogonal to the role's required ecosystem

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
